subject: Public Health
title: Wastewater Treatment
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Wastewater Treatment.

hook.text: 80% of the world's wastewater flows back into the environment untreated—polluting rivers, lakes, and oceans.

buildup.text: Wastewater carries pathogens, chemicals, pharmaceuticals, and heavy metals from homes and factories.

discovery.text: Treatment plants use biological processes—bacteria eat organic waste—followed by chemical disinfection.

twist.text: Treated wastewater can be safely reused for irrigation, industry, and even drinking water—Singapore does it.

climax.text: Investing in wastewater treatment protects downstream communities and ecosystems simultaneously.

punchline.text: One city's waste is another city's drinking water. Treatment is non-negotiable.

quiz.question: What percentage of the world's wastewater is released untreated?
